description_long: This handcart features a rustic, weathered design with a corrugated metal roof and side panels, giving it a vintage, utilitarian appearance. The main body is constructed from wood, with visible grain and a reddish-brown finish, and is supported by four metal-rimmed wheels, two larger at the rear and two smaller at the front. The cart has a pair of long, wooden handles extending from the front, designed for manual pulling or pushing. One side of the cart has a hinged, fold-down panel, likely for access or serving, while the rear includes a small step and decorative paneling. The roof is slightly overhanging, providing some shelter to the interior, which appears hollow and accessible. Metal brackets and supports reinforce the structure, and the overall build suggests it could be used for vending, transport, or display in an urban environment.
description: A vintage-style handcart with a corrugated metal roof, wooden body, and four metal-rimmed wheels, featuring a fold-down side panel and long wooden handles.
description_view_0: Side view showing the fold-down panel, long handles, and two visible wheels with the corrugated metal roof and side.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the small step, decorative rear panel, and the overhanging roof.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the side panel closed, showing the full length of the handles and wheels.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the open front area, handle ends, and the width of the roof.
attribute_wheel_position: side-mounted, two front and two rear
attribute_load_platform_design: enclosed wooden box with fold-down side
